rulings.Case Title: SHELDON T FONG VS. MICHAEL VRIHEAS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order That Requests For Admissions, Set Two, Are Deemed Admitted, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 10, 2019, Line 1, DEFENDANT CECILIA VRIHEAS' Motion For An Order That Requests For Admissions, Set Two, Are Deemed Admitted, And For Sanctions. I adopt the judge pro tem's report and recommendation on defendant Cecilia Vriheas's motion for an order that requests for admissions be deemed admitted. The motion is DENIED on the condition that responses are served before the hearing date as CCP §2033.280 provides that RFAs will not be deemed admitted if responses are served by the hearing date. If plaintiff fails to serve them by the hearing date, the motion will be granted. In any event, plaintiff is sanctioned $100 because his failure to respond necessitated the motion. (Id.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Vriheas is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JOANN WALKER VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION / Notice Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 10, 2019, Line 2, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Defendant CCSF's motion for summary adjudication is continued to May 31, 2019 pursuant to CCP 437c(h). The declaration of counsel Robinson demonstrates good cause to continue this motion. Officer Enea, one of the main witnesses in this case, has yet to be deposed. The failure to depose him was not due exclusively to plaintiff's lack of diligence but also because of the criminal trial that was pending against him. Plaintiff's supplemental opposition is due on May 20, 2019 and the supplemental reply is due on May 24, 2019. If Plaintiff seeks a further continuance of this motion, she needs to obtain a trial continuance from Department 206 first. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/RBU)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion; Declaration Of Matthew A. Brinegar
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 10, 2019, Line 4, PLAINTIFF EMILY ARREOLA, SERGIO ARREOLA's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint. Plaintiffs' motion for leave to file a first amended complaint is GRANTED. California has "a strong public policy of liberal allowance of amendments." (Mesler v. Bragg Mgmt. (1985) 290, 296.) As defendants concede, "[o]rdinarily, the judge will not consider the validity of a proposed amended pleading in deciding whether to grant leave to amend." (Opp. 2:24-26.) Trial continuances must be sought in Department 206. rulings.Case Title: EDWARD A BYRD JR VS. TURNER CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants Responses To Form Interrogatories,Set One And Special Interrogatories, Set One And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 10, 2019, Line 2, PLAINTIFF EDWARD BYRD's Motion To Compel Defendants Responses To Form Interrogatories,Set One And Special Interrogatories, Set One And For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion to compel verified responses is GRANTED as unopposed. Defendants shall provide verifications no later than May 17, 2019. The request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $60 to reimburse Plaintiff the filing fee. Defendants argue that sanctions are inappropriate under CCP § 2030.300(d) because Defendants did not oppose the motion and therefore did not "unsuccessfully ¿ oppose[] a motion to compel." While this is true for purposes of CCP § 2030.300(d), failing to provide the verifications in a timely manner, which required Plaintiff to file this motion, was a failure to confer for purposes of CCP § 2023.020. Such failure to meet and confer is a "separate offense entitling the moving party to monetary sanctions 'notwithstanding the outcome of the particular discovery motion.'" Parker v. Wolters Kluwer United States Inc. (2007) 149 Cal.App.4th 285 (citing CCP § 2023.020). Moreover, as cited in Plaintiff's reply, CRC 3.1348(a) permits the court to award sanctions even if discovery is provided or there is no opposition to the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD A BYRD JR VS. TURNER CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 10, 2019, Line 2, (part 1 of 2 tentative ruling) PLAINTIFF EDWARD BYRD's Motion To Compel Defendants Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One And For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion to compel is GRANTED as follows. The request for a privilege log is granted and must be provided no later than May 31, 2019. While Defendants provided some information about what is being withheld, by asserting privilege and work product to every request, it is unclear to which requests the objections were actually asserted. "[A] privilege log typically should provide the identity and capacity of all individuals who authored, sent, or received each allegedly privileged document, the document's date, a brief description of the document and its contents or subject matter sufficient to determine whether the privilege applies, and the precise privilege or protection asserted." Catalina Island Yacht Club v. Super. Ct. (2015) 242 Cal. App. 4th 1116, 1130; CCP § 2031.240(b)(2), (c)(1). The parties are free to agree on any presumptively privileged materials that do not need to be logged, such as communications between defendants and counsel after the filing of this complaint. If prepared "for the sole purpose of defending against claims," an incident report prepared for an insurer in anticipation of possible litigation may privileged under Soltani-Rastegar v. Super. Ct. (1989) 208 Cal. App. 3d 424, 428. However, the Site Specific Safety Program Manual provides that incident reports may be prepared for "corrective" action and to analyze incident trends (TCC 000002, 000120, 00125), which are purposes in addition to defending claims. While it is usually improper to submit new evidence on reply, this evidence undercuts Defendants' assertion that the incident report qualifies under Soltani-Rastegar. Catalina Island indicates Defendants should have an opportunity to justify the claimed privilege on the privilege log. Every response repeated objections that each request was "vague, ambiguous, and overly broad." Boilerplate objections are inappropriate and "may be sanctionable." Korea Data Systems Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1997) 51 Cal.App.4th 1513, 1516. The Court disregards these objections. [see part 2 of 2 for entire tentative ruling] =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD A BYRD JR VS. TURNER CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Edward Byrd'S Motion To Compel Defendants Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One And For Monetary Sanctions (Part 2 Of 2 For Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 10, 2019, Line 2, Plaintiff Edward Byrd'S Motion To Compel Defendants Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One And For Monetary Sanctions (Part 2 Of 2 For Tentative Ruling) Turning to specific RFPs: RFPs 1 and 2 - Unless produced by Defendants and so identified, it is unclear what Defendant identifies as responsive (or how Defendants know what Plaintiff has). Objections are overruled. RFP 3 - Despite requesting to meet and confer, it does not appear Defendants responded to Plaintiff's offer to limit the request to the pedestrian walkway where the incident occurred, between September 2013 and August 2015. Defendants did not address this in their brief. The Court adopts Plaintiff's limitation. RFPs 4 and 5 - Time records may identify witnesses and are thus appropriate subjects of discovery, so the objections are overruled. Time records may be redacted to conceal information other than name and time worked. RFPs 6, 7, 8 - Defendants should state whether the production has occurred. RFPs 9, 10, 13, 15, 18, 19, 20, 21, 23, 25, 26, 30 - The limitation to the "location of the plaintiff's fall," "location of the alleged incident" or similar is inappropriate because it may be relevant to Plaintiff's claims that there were other citations/inspections/etc. for the subject premises, not just at the specific location where the alleged fall happened. Defendants must respond to the RFPs as phrased. RFPs 11, 14, 22, 27, 28, 29, 31, 32 - Subject to any challenges based on the privilege log, the answers are sufficient. RFP 12 - It is unclear why the declarations page for the insurance policy adequately responds to the request for materials related to safety meetings related to the job site. Objections are overruled. RFPs 16, 17 - Each request to meet-and-confer is unwarranted. Each request is straightforward considering what may be relevant to the claims and defenses of any party. Objections are overruled. Defendants are ordered to provide supplemental responses consistent with this Order by May 24, 2019. Any additional production shall be completed by May 31, 2019. The request for sanctions is GRANTED. CCP § 2031.300(c). If the discovery/privilege log ordered herein is not timely served, Defendants shall pay $1,860 (6 hours at $300/hour plus filing fee) to Plaintiff by June 4, 2019. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN RIVERA VS. SENSORIA LLC. DBA DNA PIZZA AND DBA DNA LOUNGE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion: 1. For Order Establishing Admissions; And 1. For An Order For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 10, 2019, Line 3, PLAINTIFF KEVIN RIVERA's Motion: 1. For Order Establishing Admissions; And 1. For An Order For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion to deem admitted is DENIED. Before the hearing on this motion, Defendant Sensoria LLC served responses to the RFAs. CCP § 2033.280(c). An attorney may verify on behalf of a corporation, but doing so waives any attorney-client privilege or work product protection "concerning the identity of the sources of the information contained in the response." CCP § 2033.240. As such, the responses were "in substantial compliance" with the Code. CCP § 2033.280(c). The motion was filed on April 16. This was the same day on which counsel for Defendant represented that the responses would be served, and the same day on which counsel for Defendant emailed a courtesy copy of the responses to counsel for Plaintiff. The responses were formally served on April 17. Therefore, it does not appear that this motion was necessary, as Plaintiff could have been delayed a day or so to confirm that Defendant would serve responses as represented. Nevertheless, CCP § 2033.280(c) provides that it is "mandatory" that the court impose a monetary sanction when there is a motion brought to deem admitted responses to RFAs, even if the court does not deem the matters admitted. Weighing the equities, the court GRANTS the motion for sanctions in the amount of $60.00 to reimburse Plaintiff the filing fee. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KAREN BOGDANYI VS. HEALTH ADVOCATES LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 10, 2019, Line 14, DEFENDANT HEALTH ADVOCATES, LLC's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Health Advocates, LLC's demurrer is SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. The court grants Health Advocates' request for judicial notice of the pleadings and rulings in the federal and state wage and hour actions. The court finds that those documents have no probative value as they involve different actions and this court is solely concerned with whether plaintiff's first amended complaint in the instant action pleads sufficient facts to allege individual and/or class claims. The demurrer to all nine causes of action is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. "[S]imply parroting the language of [the Labor Code] in the complaint is insufficient to state a cause of action under the statute." (Hawkins v. TACA Internat. Airlines, S.A. (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 466, 478.) Plaintiff's eighth cause of action seeks relief for unfair competition. "A plaintiff alleging unfair business practices under these statutes must state with reasonable particularity the facts supporting the statutory elements of the violation." (Khoury v. Maly's of California, Inc. (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 612, 619.) Plaintiff's first amended complaint pleads simple conclusions of law that do no properly advise Health Advocates of its alleged wrongdoing. There are no facts regarding plaintiff's or class members' employment experience with Health Advocates. For this reason, the class allegations are also insufficient. Courts may resolve the issue of class treatment on demurrer. (See Tucker v. Pacific Bell Mobile Services (2012) 208 Cal.App.4th 201, 211.) The court recognizes that resolution of the class issue on the pleadings is apt to occur in a mass tort action rather than a wage and hour action. (See Tarkington v. California Unemployment Ins. Appeals Bd. (2009) 172 Cal.App.4th 1494 1511.) Nevertheless, plaintiff's current complaint is so vague that the court cannot properly conclude that class treatment is proper. Plaintiff has leave to plead facts showing an ascertainable and sufficiently numerous class and a well-defined community of interest. With regard to the community of interest requirement, plaintiff's amendments should demonstrate: (1) predominant common questions of law or fact; (2) class representatives with claims or defenses typical of the class; and (3) class representatives who can adequately represent the class. (See Ayala v. Antelope Valley Newspapers, Inc. (2014) 59 Cal.4th 522, 529-530.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Health Advocates is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MARY ELLEN PETRONI VS. NIESAR & VESTAL LLP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: For Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 10, 2019, Line 16, DEFENDANT NIESAR & VESTAL LLP, GERALD NIESAR's PETITION FOR ORDER COMPELLING ARBITRATION AND STAYING ACTION PENDING ARBITRATION Defendants Niesar & Vestal LLP and Gerald Niesar's petition to compel arbitration is GRANTED; the action is stayed pending arbitration. Whether an arbitration agreement exists is a question to be decided by the court. (Baker v. Italian Maple Holdings, LLC (2017) 13 Cal.App.5th 1152, 1158.) Niesar proves that plaintiff Mary Ellen Petroni reviewed and signed the fee agreement, confirmed the retention in a subsequent letter and made an initial payment under the hourly rates stated in the agreement. (Niesar Dec. 3-5, 7 Exs. A-B; Sbona Dec. 9-11; Earsom Dec. 2.) Petroni does not deny signing the agreement or possessing it. (Petroni Dec. 2-3.) The agreement does not unmistakably delegate issues of arbitrability or voidability to the arbitrator. (Ajamian v. CantorCO2e, L.P. (2012) 203 Cal.App.4th 771, 791.) Thus whether the agreement is voidable under Business and Professions Code §6148 is for the court to decide. Niesar's failure to get a copy of the executed agreement does not frustrate §6148's purpose, which is to ensure that the client understands and consents to the fee agreement. (National Parks & Conservation Assn. v. County of Riverside (1996) 42 Cal.App.4th 1505, 1522.) The agreement is not unenforceable as a result of Petroni not delivering an executed copy to Niesar. The agreement's language does not prescribe delivery of the contract as a condition precedent for its effect. Rather, it says attorneys "may not be able to begin work" until receiving the executed copy. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Niesar & Vestal LLP is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JONATHAN BORNSTEIN VS. DANIEL BORNSTEIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Transfer Or Alternatively For Stay Of Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 10, 2019, Line 12, DEFENDANT DANIEL BORNSTEIN, KATHRYN QUETEL, LIANA AYRAPETYAN, DANIEL CHEUNG, VICTORIA HERNANDEZ, PATRICK WOO's Motion To Transfer Or Alternatively For Stay Of Proceedings. OFF CALENDAR. Defendants failed to calendar the motion in a timely manner per CCP 1005 and plaintiff objects. Defendants only provided 16-court day notice and failed to add 2-days for e-service. Co-defendants calendared the same motion for May 17, 2019. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH ALLEN WHITE, II VS. SMULE, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 10, 2019, Line 13, DEFENDANT SMULE, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The motion to strike is ordered OFF CALENDAR to enable compliance with CCP §433.5, which requires "meet and confer in person or by telephone." Plaintiff's counsel is ordered to comply with that law. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately and Smule, Inc. has until June 10, 2019 to respond to the complaint after the meet and confer efforts. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number.=(302/RBU)
